claim: Protein foundation embeddings provide the dominant predictive signal.
evidence: ProtBERT-SW protein-only model on shared split achieved ROC-AUC 0.7274 and PR-AUC 0.7433, close to the multimodal model.

claim: Handcrafted TSS-proximal genomic features provide complementary signal.
evidence: Adding K3/K4/Basic genomic features to ProtBERT-SW improved PR-AUC to 0.7573 and MCC to 0.3438, and produced the strongest biological validation results.

claim: DNABERT-2 genomic foundation embeddings improve global ranking separation.
evidence: Combined ProtBERT-SW + DNABERT-2 achieved the highest ROC-AUC in the project: 0.7568, improving over Phase 3.1 by +0.0278.

claim: DNABERT-2 does not fully replace the handcrafted genomic branch in the current study.
evidence: Phase 3.1 retained higher PR-AUC and MCC and stronger top-50 biological validation than Phase 5.3.

claim: The final framework is biologically plausible.
evidence: Top true positives and top 50 Phase 3 predictions were enriched for oxidative phosphorylation, respiratory chain complex I, insulin resistance, type II diabetes mellitus, insulin secretion, and pancreas development.
